name: Namibia (Republic of Namibia)
summary: Namibia is a Southern African mixed-legal-system republic combining Roman-Dutch civil-law substantive heritage (via South African Cape Colony administration) with English common-law procedural inheritance and customary-law personal-status jurisdiction via Community Courts (under the Community Courts Act 10/2003). Family-law framework operates under the Child Care and Protection Act 3/2015, Marriage Act 25/1961, Married Persons Equality Act 1/1996, and Recognition of Customary Marriages Bill (pending). Parental responsibility and child custody are governed by Child Care and Protection Act Parts III-IV. The Supreme Court of Namibia is the apex court for civil and criminal matters. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the High Court (Family Division) and Magistrates' Courts. Psychology profession is regulated through the Health Professions Councils of Namibia under the Allied Health Professions Council framework with the Psychology Association of Namibia operating professional standards. Namibia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the best-interests-of-the-child principle codified in Child Care and Protection Act s. 3. Namibia is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.title: Child Care and Protection Act — Parental responsibility and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Child Care and Protection Act codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 3), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions.

statutory_framework.title: Married Persons Equality Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute on marital equality and parental rights post-independence.

statutory_framework.title: Community Courts Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal statute establishing Community Courts for customary-law jurisdiction.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Namibia
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

professional_regulators.name: Allied Health Professions Council of Namibia
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of allied health professionals including clinical psychology under Health Professions Councils framework.

professional_regulators.name: Psychology Association of Namibia
professional_regulators.role: Peak professional association for psychologists in Namibia.

anonymisation_convention: Namibian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Herero and Namaqua genocide + German-colonial framework
key_developments.description: Herero and Namaqua genocide 1904-1908 by German Empire — substantively distinctive globally first-genocide-of-20th-century framework with estimated 80% of Herero and 50% of Namaqua populations killed. Foundational pre-South-African-administration framework substantively affecting subsequent demographic and constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: South African Mandate + League-of-Nations-Mandate + South-West-Africa framework
key_developments.description: South African Mandate over South-West Africa established 17 December 1920 under League of Nations Mandate framework. Subsequent UN-revocation 1966 + ongoing-South-African-administration 1920-1990 substantively distinctive Southern-African 70-year-administration framework. Foundational pre-independence framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Namibian War of Independence + SWAPO + Cuito Cuanavale framework
key_developments.description: Namibian War of Independence (South African Border War) 26 August 1966 – 21 March 1990 — SWAPO (South West Africa People's Organisation) led by Sam Nujoma. Substantive Battle of Cuito Cuanavale 1987-1988 substantively significant Cold-War-era African armed-conflict framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Independence and Constitution + Sam Nujoma founding-presidency
key_developments.description: Namibian independence 21 March 1990 from South African administration — Sam Nujoma first president 1990-2005. Constitution of Namibia 1990 adopted with retention of Roman-Dutch civil-law heritage. Substantively distinctive Southern African last-major-decolonisation framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Namibia ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Namibia ratified the UNCRC on 30 September 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2015 Child Care and Protection Act substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Married Persons Equality Act
key_developments.description: Federal statute on marital equality and parental rights post-independence — Act 1/1996 substantively reforming colonial-era marital framework. Substantively distinctive Southern African post-apartheid marital-equality framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Community Courts Act
key_developments.description: Federal statute establishing Community Courts for customary-law jurisdiction Act 10/2003 — substantively significant Southern African customary-law-recognition framework. Foundational substantive-customary-law framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Child Care and Protection Act
key_developments.description: Federal Child Care and Protection Act 3/2015 codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 3), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection. Substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation within Southern African mixed-legal-system framework.

key_developments.title: German genocide-recognition + reparations-framework + 1.1-billion-euro-package
key_developments.description: Germany formally recognised Herero and Namaqua genocide May 2021 — substantively distinctive globally first-formal-acknowledgement-by-perpetrator-state framework. Substantive €1.1 billion reconciliation package framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and historical-reparations framework.

key_developments.title: Ombudsman + Children's Advocate institutional framework + Geingob presidency final-year
key_developments.description: Hage Geingob (SWAPO) presidency final-year 2023 framework (Geingob died 4 February 2024 in office). Office of the Ombudsman and Children's Advocate institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Child Care and Protection Act 2015 + Married Persons Equality Act 1996 + Community Courts Act 2003 framework. Substantively significant Southern African institutional jurisprudential development establishing trajectory toward 2024 first-female-president framework.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of Namibia + Community Courts — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah presidency
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of Namibia and Community Courts continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Child Care and Protection Act 2015 s. 3 + Married Persons Equality Act 1996 + Community Courts Act 2003 framework in custody disputes. Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah (SWAPO) elected President November 2024 — substantively distinctive Southern African first-female-president of Namibia framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Namibia operates a structurally distinctive mixed-legal-system framework — Roman-Dutch civil-law substantive heritage (via South African Cape Colony administration) + English common-law procedural + customary-law personal-status via Community Courts. Within the Roman-Dutch substantive tradition cluster alongside South Africa, Lesotho, Botswana, Zimbabwe, Sri Lanka.
Independence-era civil-law-retention pattern shared with South Africa is structurally distinctive — Roman-Dutch heritage explicitly retained at independence rather than replaced.
Non-Hague Convention status places Namibia in the non-Hague Southern African cluster.
Child Care and Protection Act 2015 is among the more recent comprehensive children's-protection statutes within the African corpus cluster.

sources.title: Allied Health Professions Council of Namibia

editorial_notes: Namibia jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 4 to 10 key_developments with full Herero-Namaqua-genocide-to-Nandi-Ndaitwah trajectory: 1904-Herero-and-Namaqua-genocide + 1920-South-African-Mandate-+-League-of-Nations-Mandate + 1966-Namibian-War-of-Independence-+-SWAPO + 1990-Independence-and-Constitution-+-Sam-Nujoma + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1996-Married-Persons-Equality-Act + 2003-Community-Courts-Act + 2015-Child-Care-and-Protection-Act + 2021-German-genocide-recognition-+-reparations + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-Community-Courts-+-Nandi-Ndaitwah-first-female-president.

Mixed-legal-system Southern Africa (Roman-Dutch substantive + English common-law procedural + customary-law personal-status). Child Care and Protection Act 2015 + Married Persons Equality Act 1996 + Community Courts Act 2003 + non-Hague Convention.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Child Care and Protection Act 2015 s. 3 + Married Persons Equality Act + customary-law framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Southern-African + Roman-Dutch + mixed-legal-system + Herero-and-Namaqua-genocide-1904-1908-first-genocide-of-20th-century-globally-distinctive-80-percent-Herero-50-percent-Namaqua + South-African-Mandate-1920-1990-70-year-administration + Namibian-War-of-Independence-1966-1990-+-SWAPO-+-Cuito-Cuanavale-1987-1988 + Sam-Nujoma-founding-president-1990-2005 + last-major-decolonisation-1990 + Married-Persons-Equality-Act-1996-post-apartheid-marital-equality + Community-Courts-Act-2003-customary-law + Child-Care-and-Protection-Act-2015 + German-genocide-recognition-2021-first-formal-acknowledgement-by-perpetrator-state-distinctive + Nandi-Ndaitwah-first-female-president-2024 + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus. 🎯 100TH JURISDICTION MILESTONE.
